    <h1 {...{
      "id": "use-pdf-and-powerpoint"
    }}>{`Use PDF and PowerPoint`}</h1>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "import-pdf-into-the-page"
    }}>{`Import PDF into the page`}</h2>
    <p>{`You can add PDFs to the page using the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../references/addonsdk/app-document.md#importpdf"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`importPdf()`}</inlineCode></a>{` method of the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`addOnUISdk.app.document`}</inlineCode>{` object, which expects a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Blob`}</inlineCode>{` object as an argument and a `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../references/addonsdk/app-document.md#mediaattributes"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`MediaAttribute`}</inlineCode></a>{` object with a title (mandatory) and author (optional) as the second.`}</p>
    <p>{`PDF and PowerPoint imports will trigger a consent dialog that asks the user to confirm the process; it's not possible to bypass it. As soon as the process starts, another dialog will preview the PDF and track the operation progress.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert slots="text" variant="info" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`The SDK shows a built-in progress dialog; there is no progress callback exposed.`}</p>

    <p>{`Supported vector elements will be kept editable (e.g., shapes with rounded corners, text, etc.), and all pages will be imported.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example"
    }}>{`Example`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`import addOnUISdk from "https://express.adobe.com/static/add-on-sdk/sdk.js";

addOnUISdk.ready.then(async () => {
  try {
    const pdfUrl = "https://url/to/your/file.pdf";

    const pdf = await fetch(pdfUrl);
    const pdfBlob = await pdf.blob();

    await addOnUISdk.app.document.importPdf(
      pdfBlob, // 👈 Blob object
      {
        title: "Official Launch Party",
        author: "Adobe",
      }
    );
  } catch (e) {
    console.error("Failed to add the PDF", e);
  }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Please note that you can use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fetch()`}</inlineCode>{` also to get PDFs that are local to the add-on; in this case, you can use paths relative to the add-on's root.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`import addOnUISdk from "https://express.adobe.com/static/add-on-sdk/sdk.js";

addOnUISdk.ready.then(async () => {
  try {
    // 👇 Local PDF
     const pdfUrl = "./OfficialLaunchParty.pdf";
    const pdf = await fetch(pdfUrl);
    // ... same as before
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "importing-converted-documents"
    }}>{`Importing converted documents`}</h3>
    <p>{`If your add-on converts Word documents (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.docx`}</inlineCode>{`) or Google Docs (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.gdoc`}</inlineCode>{`) to PDF before importing, you can use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`sourceMimeType`}</inlineCode>{` parameter to improve the user experience. When specified, the import consent dialog displays the message "Import a document" rather than the default "Import a PDF".`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Import a PDF that was converted from a Word document
await addOnUISdk.app.document.importPdf(convertedPdfBlob, {
  title: "Converted Document",
  sourceMimeType: "docx" // Shows "Import a document" in the dialog
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "import-presentation-into-the-page"
    }}>{`Import presentation into the page`}</h2>
    <p>{`For PowerPoint files, the process is similar to the one for PDFs, but use the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../references/addonsdk/app-document.md#importpresentation"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`importPresentation()`}</inlineCode></a>{` method instead. Express supports only the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.pptx`}</inlineCode>{` format; the legacy `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.ppt`}</inlineCode>{` format is not supported. The method shows the same consent and progress dialog as for PDF import.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`import addOnUISdk from "https://express.adobe.com/static/add-on-sdk/sdk.js";

addOnUISdk.ready.then(async () => {
  try {
    const powerPointUrl = "https://url/to/your/file.pptx";
    // Or for local files: const powerPointUrl = "./OfficialLaunchParty.pptx";
    // Note: Only .pptx is supported; .ppt is not supported.

    const powerPoint = await fetch(powerPointUrl);
    const powerPointBlob = await powerPoint.blob();

    await addOnUISdk.app.document.importPresentation(
      powerPointBlob, // 👈 Blob object
      {
        title: "Official Launch Party",
        author: "Adobe",
      }
    );
  } catch (e) {
    console.error("Failed to add the presentation", e);
  }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "presentation-import-limitations-and-tips"
    }}>{`Presentation import limitations and tips`}</h3>
    <p>{`When importing presentations (such as PowerPoint `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.pptx`}</inlineCode>{` files) into Adobe Express, the file is converted into an editable Express format. During conversion, complex elements may be removed, flattened, or converted to static content.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "limitations-on-imported-content"
    }}>{`Limitations on imported content`}</h4>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Animations:`}</strong>{` Advanced PowerPoint animations, triggers, and custom entrance/exit effects are generally not supported. Basic transitions may be lost or simplified.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Audio and Video:`}</strong>{` Embedded audio and video in the original presentation may fail to import or play.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Formatting and Fonts:`}</strong>{` Custom fonts, specialized shapes, and complex layouts may render differently after import.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "tips-for-successful-import"
    }}>{`Tips for successful import`}</h4>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Convert to PDF first:`}</strong>{` For stronger visual fidelity, save the presentation as a PDF before importing. Keep in mind this approach can make content less editable.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Simplify before import:`}</strong>{` Remove complex animations and embedded media in the source presentation to reduce conversion issues.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Re-add media in Express:`}</strong>{` After import, upload audio/video directly in Adobe Express for better compatibility.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Prefer H.264 video:`}</strong>{` If video imports, H.264-encoded media is typically more stable.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`If import fails entirely, check file size and unsupported elements. For current size limits and supported import details, see `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://helpx.adobe.com/express/web/bring-in-assets-from-other-apps/import-files-from-other-apps/import-files.html"
      }}>{`Import files from other apps`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "faqs"
    }}>{`FAQs`}</h2>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-how-do-i-import-pdf-files"
    }}>{`Q: How do I import PDF files?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`addOnUISdk.app.document.importPdf(blob, attributes)`}</inlineCode>{` with PDF blob and MediaAttribute object.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-how-do-i-import-powerpoint-files"
    }}>{`Q: How do I import PowerPoint files?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`addOnUISdk.app.document.importPresentation(blob, attributes)`}</inlineCode>{` with PowerPoint blob and MediaAttribute.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-what-powerpoint-formats-are-supported"
    }}>{`Q: What PowerPoint formats are supported?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Only the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.pptx`}</inlineCode>{` format is supported. The legacy `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.ppt`}</inlineCode>{` format is not supported.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-are-mediaattributes-required-for-pdfpowerpoint"
    }}>{`Q: Are MediaAttributes required for PDF/PowerPoint?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Yes, title is mandatory and author is optional in the MediaAttribute object.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-what-is-the-sourcemimetype-parameter-for"
    }}>{`Q: What is the sourceMimeType parameter for?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`sourceMimeType`}</inlineCode>{` in MediaAttributes to improve UX when importing converted documents. It shows "Import a document" instead of "Import a PDF" in the consent dialog.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-when-should-i-use-sourcemimetype"
    }}>{`Q: When should I use sourceMimeType?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Use it when importing PDFs that were converted from other document types like Word (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.docx`}</inlineCode>{`) or Google Docs (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.gdoc`}</inlineCode>{`) to provide clearer messaging to users.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-what-values-does-sourcemimetype-accept"
    }}>{`Q: What values does sourceMimeType accept?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Common values include "docx" for Word documents and "gdoc" for Google Docs. Use the original document format before PDF conversion.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-will-users-see-a-consent-dialog"
    }}>{`Q: Will users see a consent dialog?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Yes, PDF and PowerPoint imports trigger consent dialogs that users must confirm.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-can-i-bypass-the-consent-dialog"
    }}>{`Q: Can I bypass the consent dialog?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` No, the consent dialog cannot be bypassed for PDF and PowerPoint imports.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-are-vector-elements-preserved"
    }}>{`Q: Are vector elements preserved?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Yes, supported vector elements like shapes and text remain editable after import.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-how-many-pages-are-imported"
    }}>{`Q: How many pages are imported?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` All pages from PDF and PowerPoint files are imported into the document.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-what-are-the-file-size-limits-for-importing-pdfs-and-powerpoint-files"
    }}>{`Q: What are the file size limits for importing PDFs and PowerPoint files?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` For current file size limits and detailed import requirements, see `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://helpx.adobe.com/express/web/bring-in-assets-from-other-apps/import-files-from-other-apps/import-files.html"
      }}>{`Import files from other apps`}</a>{` in the Express help documentation.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-why-did-animations-audio-or-video-get-lost-after-importing-a-presentation"
    }}>{`Q: Why did animations, audio, or video get lost after importing a presentation?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` During conversion to Express format, advanced animations and embedded media may not be supported. Simplify the source file before import and re-add media directly in Adobe Express for better compatibility. See `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "#presentation-import-limitations-and-tips"
      }}>{`Presentation import limitations and tips`}</a>{`.`}</p>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
;
MDXContent.isMDXComponent = true;
